summary: omnius ships postinstall daemon performing host enumeration and network exfiltration
details: The package's package.json declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks and ships dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs, a CommonJS file that imports os, fs, http, and child_process and uses them together at install time. The daemon calls os.userInfo() and execSync() to capture host/user identifiers and shell command output, runs ping and similar network probes, and issues outbound http.request GETs from the same module — the structural shape of an installer-side host-enumeration and exfiltration beacon. package.json also embeds curl invocations in lifecycle scripts. There is no documented purpose for a long-lived 'postinstall daemon' that gathers user identity and probes the network on every npm install. Installing this package executes the daemon against the installer's machine without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
